extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates matters against the Company, by the Company, against/by subsidiaries, and matters involving Directors and Promoters. I counted only against rows: criminal proceedings 3 against the Company + 1 against subsidiaries + 2 against Directors + 2 against Promoters = 8; statutory/regulatory actions 1 + 1 + 1 = 3; and tax proceedings against the Company and subsidiary, comprising direct tax 5 + 1 and indirect tax 3 = 9. For the quantified group litigation amount, I used the against-Company and against-Subsidiaries rows only: ₹0.60 million + ₹20.38 million + ₹23.46 million + ₹150.00 million + ₹237.15 million = ₹431.59 million, excluding “Not ascertainable” amounts; converted from million to crore at 10 million per crore, this is ₹43.159 crore. The contingent-liabilities risk states only that contingent liabilities existed as of March 31, 2019 and provides no Total figure in the supplied text. No company-wide top-five or top-ten customer concentration percentage is disclosed; the only percentages are unrelated employee expenses, mobile traffic and other metrics.
